mission.title: distribution: Add config support for limiting allowed mediaTypes
mission.description:
Fixes #2928 (please see issue for details).

Added docs and tests. Let me know if any changes required. Thank you!

resolution.summary:
Adds page for projects or companies currently engaged in efforts to
support OCI artifacts.

Hi, any update?
@jdolitsky thx for this PR, lots to review, seems well thought out :-) @dmcgowan 
@mikebrow yep, definitely! Let me know if any changes required, happy to do so
Not a maintainer here, so just a drive-by observation 🤗


It looks like there's a subtle, but possibly important difference between and URL allow/deny rules;

For URLs:

> If allow is `unset`, pushing a manifest containing URLs fails.

For mediaTypes:

> If allow is unset, pushing a manifest containing any mediaType in the config succeeds (all config mediaTypes allowed).


I assume this is for backward compatibility, but as a result won't allow the option of configuring a _whitelist_ (deny "everything", and _allow_ a specific list of media types)

- Would that be an important use-case?
- To facilitate that use-case, would perhaps making a distinction between "no set" and "set, but empty list" work?

Another thing I looked at was "regular expressions" in the initial implementation. I'm not a maintainer for this project, but I tend to prefer keeping things simple (in this case; just a list of mediatypes), unless it's critical to make the feature useful. (Usually, it's easier to _add_ functionality (eg separate `allowMatch` rule) than to _remove_ once added).

I can see it being consistent with existing allow/deny rules, so that may be the reason for using regular expressions; just curious;

- If there's actual use-cases where regular expressions are _required_ for this functionality (the apples/bananas example is fictive, so doesn't give a clue for a real-life scenario), or
- if regular expressions add _significant_ benefits for the feature


@caervs -

> Do you think it would make sense as a follow-up to this PR for the service to block blob uploads for media types that aren't valid as configs or layers?

yes, let's.

in terms of the stuff in handlers/app.go - this was mostly copying what had been done for the other section of the config. will take care of the docs fix.

---

@thaJeztah - 

I was troubled by trying to apply regex to the media types.. I would honestly rather have just a static list as a distribution deploy-er. I'm not sure if its better to follow the convention in the config or do something custom. what do you think? @caervs?

something maybe like
